justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Timeout mismatch clearly identified with explicit computation 200+150+800=1150ms > 500ms. (2) Orphaned downstream request consequence thoroughly described — service continues working after upstream gives up, wasting CPU/network/threads. (3) Outbound > inbound relationship explicitly called out as 'unambiguous misconfiguration' (1000ms each vs 500ms inbound). (4) Multiple remediation options provided: parallelization, timeout reduction to ≤450ms, graceful degradation, circuit breakers, context/deadline propagation. (5) Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Exceptional depth — goes well beyond the rubric minimum by computing not just the aggregate mismatch but breaking down the budget exhaustion step-by-step across each sequential call
Correctly identifies InventoryService p99 (800ms) alone exceeds the 500ms inbound timeout, making the mismatch unavoidable even if calls were reordered
Identifies the cascading failure mode: slow downstream + no circuit breakers → connection pool exhaustion → total service outage, which is the second-order consequence beyond simple timeout mismatch
Correctly notes that absence of retries is actually appropriate given the tight budget — shows nuanced understanding rather than reflexively recommending retries
Recommends context/deadline propagation to abort orphaned downstream calls, which directly addresses the wasted-work consequence
Provides concrete timeout values (≤450ms per parallel call) rather than vague 'reduce timeouts' advice
Flags the health check design issue (liveness vs readiness) which is an operational subtlety beyond the rubric scope
